classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “These words spake Jesus in the treasury, as He taught in the Temple; and no man laid hands on Him, for His hour was not yet come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Oh the folly of the Jews! seeking Him as they did before the Passover, and then having found Him in the midst of them, and having often attempted to take Him by their own or by others’ hands without being able; they were not even so awed by His power, but set themselves to their wickedness, and desisted not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it saith, that they continually made the attempt; “These words spake He in the treasury, teaching in the Temple; and no man laid hands on Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He spake in the Temple, and in the character of teacher, which was more adapted to rouse them, and He spake those things because of which they were stung, and charged Him with making Himself equal to the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “the witness of two men is true,” proveth this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet still “He spake these words,” It saith, “in the Temple,” in the character of teacher, “and no man laid hands on Him, for His hour was not yet come”; that is, it was not yet the fitting time at which He would be crucified.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that even then the deed done was not of their power, but of His dispensation, for they had long desired, but had not been able, nor would they even then have been able, except He had consented.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then said Jesus unto them, I go My way, and ye shall seek Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why saith He this continually?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To shame and terrify their souls; for observe what fear this saying caused in them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Although they desired to kill Him that they might be rid of Him, they yet ask, “whither He goeth,” such great things did they imagine from the matter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He desired also to show them another thing, that the deed would not be effected through their force; but He showed it to them in a figure beforehand, and already foretold the Resurrection by these words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then said the Jews, Will he kill himself?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then doth Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To remove their suspicion, and to show that such an act is sin, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye are from beneath.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He saith, is of this kind: “It is no wonder that ye imagine such things, ye who are carnal men, and have no spiritual thoughts, but I shall not do anything of the kind, for,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I am from above; ye are of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here again He speaketh of their worldly and carnal imaginations, whence it is clear that the, “I am not of this world,” doth not mean that He had not taken upon Him flesh, but that He was far removed from their wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He even saith, that His disciples were “not of the world” ( c. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 19 ), yet they had flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As then Paul, when he saith, “Ye are not in the flesh” ( Rom. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 9 ), doth not mean that they are incorporeal, so Christ when He saith, that His disciples are “not of the world,” doth nothing else than testify to their heavenly wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I said therefore unto you that…if ye believe not that I am He, ye shall die in your sins.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if He came to take away the sin of the world, and if it is impossible for men to put that off in any other way except by the washing, it needs must be that he that believeth not must depart hence, having the old man; since he that will not by faith slay and bury that old man, shall die in him, and shall go away to that place to suffer the punishment of His former sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He said, “He that believeth not is judged already” ( c. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 18 ); not merely through his not believing, but because he de parteth parteth hence having his former sins upon him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then said they unto Him, Who art thou?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: After so long a time, such signs and teaching, they ask, “Who art thou?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The same that I told you from the beginning.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He saith, is of this kind; “Ye are not worthy to hear My words at all, much less to learn who I am, for ye say all that ye do, tempting Me, and giving heed to none of My sayings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And all this I could now prove against you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is the sense of,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I have many things to say and to judge of you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I could not only prove you guilty, but also punish you; but He that sent Me, that is, the Father, willeth not this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For I am come not to judge the world, but to save the world, since God sent not His Son to judge the world, He saith, but to save the world. ( c. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If now He hath sent Me for this, and He is true, with good cause I judge no one now.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But these things I speak that are for your salvation, not what are for your condemnation.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He speaketh thus, lest they should deem that it was through weakness that on hearing so much from them He went not to extremities, or that He knew not their secret thoughts and scoffings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They understood not that He spake to them of the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He ceased not to speak concerning Him, and they knew Him not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then when after working many signs, and teaching them, He drew them not to Himself, He next speaketh to them of the Cross, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “When ye have lifted up the Son of Man, then ye shall know that I Am, and that I speak not of Myself, and that He that sent Me is with Me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the Father hath not left Me alone.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He showeth that He rightly said, “the same that I said unto you from the beginning.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So little heed they gave to His words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “When ye have lifted up the Son of Man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Do ye not expect that ye then shall certainly rid yourselves of Me, and slay Me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But I tell you that then ye shall most know that I Am, by reason of the miracles, the resurrection, and the destruction (of Jerusalem).”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For all these things were sufficient to manifest His power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “Then ye shall know who I am”; for, “when ye shall see,” He saith, “that I suffer nothing from death, then ye shall know that I Am, that is, the Christ, the Son of God, who govern all things, and am not opposed to Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For which cause He addeth, “and of Myself I speak nothing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For ye shall know both My power and My unanimity with the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because the, “of Myself I speak nothing,” showeth that His Substance differeth not (from that of the Father), and that He uttereth nothing save that which is in the mind of the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For when ye have been driven away from your place of worship, and it is not allowed you even to serve Him as hitherto, then ye shall know that He doth this to avenge Me, and because He is wroth with those who would not hear Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As though He had said, “Had I been an enemy and a stranger to God, He would not have stirred up such wrath against you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This also Esaias declareth, “He shall give the wicked in return for His burial” ( Isa. liii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 9 , LXX.); and David, “Then shall He speak unto them in His wrath” ( Ps. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 5 ); and Christ Himself, “Behold, your house is left unto you desolate.” ( Matt. xxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And His parables declare the same thing when He saith, “What shall the Lord of that vineyard do to those husbandmen?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He shall miserably destroy those wicked men.” ( Matt. xxi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that everywhere He speaketh thus, because He is not yet believed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if He will destroy them, as He will, (for, “Bring hither,” It saith, “those which would not that I should reign over them, and slay them,”) wherefore saith He that the deed is not His, but His Father’s?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He addresseth Himself to their weakness, and at the same time honoreth Him that begat Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He said not, “I leave your house desolate,” but, it “is left”; He hath put it impersonally.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But by saying, “How often would I have gathered your children together—and ye would not,” and then adding, “is left,” He showeth that He wrought the desolation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For since,” He telleth them, “when ye were benefited and healed of your infirmities, ye would not know Me, ye shall know by being punished who I am.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And the Father is with Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That they may not deem the “who sent Me” to be a mark of inferiority, He saith, “is with Me”; the first belongeth to the Dispensation, the second to the Godhead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And He hath not left Me alone,” for I do always those things that please Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again He hath brought down His discourse to a humbler strain, continually setting Himself against that which they asserted, that He was not of God, and that He kept not the Sabbath.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To this He replieth, “I do always those things that are pleasing unto Him”; showing that it was pleasing unto Him even that the Sabbath should be broken.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So, for instance, just before the Crucifixion He said, “Think ye that I cannot call upon My Father?” ( Matt. xxvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet by merely saying, “Whom seek ye?” ( c. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 4, 6 ) He cast them down backwards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then saith He not, “Think ye that I cannot destroy you,” when He had proved this by deed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He condescendeth to their infirmity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He took great pains to show that He did nothing contrary to the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus He speaketh rather after the manner of a man; and as “He hath not left Me alone,” was spoken, so also was the, “I do always those things that are pleasing unto Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “As He spake these words, many believed on Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When He brought down His speech to a lowly strain, many believed on Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou still ask wherefore He speaketh humbly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet the Evangelist clearly alluded to this when he said, “As He spake these things, many believed on Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By this all but proclaiming aloud to us, “Oh hearer, be not confounded if thou hear any lowly expression, for they who after such high teaching were not yet persuaded that He was of the Father, were with good reason made to hear humbler words, that they might believe.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this is an excuse for those things which shall be spoken in a humble way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They believed then, yet not as they ought, but carelessly and as it were by chance, being pleased and refreshed by the humility of the words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that they had not perfect faith the Evangelist shows by their speeches after this, in which they insult Him again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that these are the very same persons he has declared by saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then said Jesus to those Jews which believed on Him, If ye continue in My word.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Showing that they had not yet received His doctrine, but only gave heed unto His words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He speaketh more sharply.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Before He merely said, “Ye shall seek Me” ( c. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 34 ), but now He addeth what is more, “Ye shall die in your sins.” ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He showeth how; “because ye cannot when ye are come to that place afterwards entreat Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “These things which I speak unto the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By these words He showed that He was now going forth to the Gentiles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But because they still knew not that He spake to them of the Father, He again speaketh of Him, and the Evangelist hath put the reason of the humility of the expressions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If now we will thus search the Scriptures, exactly and not carelessly, we shall be able to attain unto our salvation; if we continually dwell upon them, we shall learn right doctrine and a perfect life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For although a man be very hard, and stubborn, and proud, and profit nothing at other times, yet at least he shall gain fruit from this time, and receive benefit, if not so great as to admit of his being sensible of it, still he shall receive it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if a man who passes by an ointment-maker’s shop, or sitteth in one, is impregnated with the perfume even against his will, much more is this the case with one who cometh to church.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as idleness is born of idleness, so too from working is generated a ready mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Although thou art full of ten thousand sins, although thou art impure, shun not the tarrying here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Wherefore,” it may be said, “when hearing I do not?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is no small profit to deem one’s self wretched; this fear is not useless, this dread is not unseasonable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If only thou groanest that, “hearing I do not,” thou wilt certainly come also to the doing at some time or other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it cannot be that he who speaks with God, and hears God speak, should not profit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We compose ourselves at once and wash our hands when we desire to take the Bible into them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou even before the reading what reverence is here?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if we go on with exactness, we shall reap great advantage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For we should not, unless it served to place the soul in reverence, have washed our hands; and a woman if she be unveiled straightway puts on her veil, giving proof of internal reverence, and a man if he be covered bares his head.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how the outward behavior proclaims the inward reverence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then moreover he that sits to hear groans often, and condemns his present life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then, beloved, give heed to the Scriptures, and if no other part be so, let the Gospels at least be the subjects of our earnest care, let us keep them in our hands.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For straightway when thou hast opened the Book thou shalt see the name of Christ there, and shalt hear one say, “The birth of Jesus Christ was on this wise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When His mother Mary was espoused to Joseph, she was found with Child of the Holy Ghost.” ( Matt. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He that heareth this will immediately desire virginity, will marvel at the Birth, will be freed from earthly things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is not a little thing when thou seest the Virgin deemed worthy of the Spirit, and an Angel talking with her.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this upon the very surface; but if thou perseverest to go on unto the end, thou shalt loathe all that pertains to this life, shalt mock at all worldly things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If thou art rich, thou shalt think nothing of wealth, when thou hearest that she who was (the wife) of a carpenter, and of humble family, became the mother of thy Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If thou art poor thou shalt not be ashamed of thy poverty, when thou hearest that the Creator of the world was not ashamed of the meanest dwelling.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Considering this, thou wilt not rob, thou wilt not covet, thou wilt not take the goods of others, but wilt rather be a lover of poverty, and despise wealth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if this be the case, thou shalt banish all evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, when thou seest Him lying in a manger, thou wilt not be anxious to put golden garments about thy child, or to cause thy wife’s couch to be inlaid with silver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if thou carest not for these things, thou wilt not do either the deeds of covetousness and rapine, which are caused by them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Many other things you may gain which I cannot separately enumerate, but they will know who have made the trial.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore I exhort you both to obtain Bibles, and to retain together with the Bibles the sentiments they set forth, and to write them in your minds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Jews because they gave no heed were commanded to suspend their books from their hands; but we place them not even in our hands but in our house, when we ought to stamp them on our heart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus cleansing our present life, we shall obtain the good things that are to come to which may we all attain, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, now and ever, and world without end.
